content.claim: The 2017–18 'lex Taigetosz' reform (amendment to Public Education Act, effective September 2018) ended exemption from grading/classification for BTMN students while retaining it for SNI students, restructuring the legal incentive between the two categories.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The causal link between this legal tightening and subsequent SNI numerical rise is asserted by journalists but not established by controlled research
No quantitative before-after study isolates the reform's effect from other concurrent factors (COVID, diagnostic-practice evolution, environmental stressors)
TASZ objection documents predicted harm but predates the observed acceleration, so ex-post validation is circumstantial
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
